fact: The first official cricket Test match was played in 1877 between Australia and England at the Melbourne Cricket Ground (MCG). Australia won by 45 runs.
Cricket legend Don Bradman retired with a Test batting average of 99.94, the highest batting average in the history of Test cricket.
Sachin Tendulkar holds the record for the most international centuries, scoring 100 centuries across Test and ODI formats during his career.
Muttiah Muralitharan of Sri Lanka is the highest wicket-taker in Test cricket history, concluding his career with exactly 800 wickets.
The first Men's Cricket World Cup was held in England in 1975, with the West Indies defeating Australia in the final to lift the trophy.
The Thomas Cup, established in 1948, is the premier international men's team badminton championship. India won its historic first title in 2022 by defeating Indonesia 3-0.
Badminton was introduced as an official Olympic sport at the 1992 Barcelona Summer Games. China has historically been the dominant nation in Olympic badminton.
Lin Dan of China is considered one of the greatest players ever, being the first to complete the 'Super Grand Slam' by winning all nine major titles.
The fastest smash hit in competitive badminton was recorded by India's Satwiksairaj Rankireddy in 2023, clocking an incredible 565 km/h (351 mph).
The Uber Cup is the women's equivalent of the Thomas Cup, first held in 1956-1957. China holds the record for the most Uber Cup titles.
The FIFA World Cup was first held in 1930. Uruguay hosted and won the tournament, defeating Argentina 4-2 in the final match in Montevideo.
Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup a record five times (1958, 1962, 1970, 1994, 2002), making them the most successful nation in World Cup history.
Pelé is the only football player in history to win three FIFA World Cups, achieving the feat with Brazil in 1958, 1962, and 1970.
Lionel Messi holds the record for winning the most Ballon d'Or awards, given to the best player in the world, securing the honor eight times.
Real Madrid is the most successful club in UEFA Champions League history, having won the prestigious European trophy more than 14 times.
Wimbledon is the oldest tennis tournament in the world, founded in 1877. It is the only Grand Slam still played on traditional grass courts.
Margaret Court holds the all-time record for the most Grand Slam women's singles titles, having won 24 major championships during her career.
The longest tennis match in history occurred at Wimbledon in 2010, where John Isner defeated Nicolas Mahut after 11 hours and 5 minutes of play.
In 1988, Steffi Graf achieved the Golden Slam by winning all four Grand Slam titles and the Olympic gold medal in a single calendar year.
Rafael Nadal is known as the 'King of Clay' due to his unprecedented dominance at the French Open, winning the tournament a record 14 times.
Basketball was invented by Dr. James Naismith in 1891 in Springfield, Massachusetts. The first game was played with a soccer ball and peach baskets.
Michael Jordan won six NBA Championships with the Chicago Bulls in the 1990s and was named the NBA Finals MVP in all six of those series.
Wilt Chamberlain holds the record for the most points scored in a single NBA game, dropping exactly 100 points for the Philadelphia Warriors in 1962.
In 2023, LeBron James officially surpassed Kareem Abdul-Jabbar to become the all-time leading scorer in NBA regular-season history.
The International Basketball Federation (FIBA) was founded in Geneva in 1932 to govern international competition, initially focusing on amateur players.
